rationale: The citing paper uses Ng, Faraji-Rad, and Batra (2021) not just in a general literature list, but also in a direct, claim-specific way to justify how global-local identity should be conceptualized and operationalized (relative identity strength). It also relies on that work as part of the framing that identity salience can shift consumer preferences. This is more than a passing mention, even though the citing paper does not reuse the cited paper's uncertainty-to-thinking-style mechanism.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as part of the conceptual and empirical basis for global-local identity effects in consumption and explicitly draws on it to support a relative (unidimensional) identity operationalization. It then builds a new account linking global-local identity to access-based consumption via consumption openness.
